title: Rinorea condensa
taxonomy: Rinorea condensa belongs to the family Violaceae, commonly known as the violet family. This classification places Rinorea condensa within the order Malpighiales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Violaceae is a small family of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Rinorea condensa is a genus of flowering plants that are native to Southeast Asia. The exact classification of Rinorea condensa may vary depending on the taxonomic source, but it is generally agreed upon as a member of the family Violaceae.
